purpose: Attack the source-law preimage candidate by replacing zero-twisted residual orientation with weaker, swapped, shifted, and older substitute predicates.
boundary: This does not close Gap A.
The tested object is a source-law preimage candidate, not a full source-derived chamber/register map.
The zero-twisted source currently uses the recorded zero-split fallback unless direct lift provenance is found.
Surviving exact substitutes, if any, prevent promotion.
No global exact selector is claimed.

baseline.rule: zero-twisted AND (singleton ZT OR multi-column TI)

interpretation.main_result: The source-law preimage candidate remains exact as baseline.
interpretation.weaker_nulls: Zero-twisted alone, residual orientation alone, stable orientation, role-only, shape-only, and shifted zero-source nulls are tested.
interpretation.survivor_warning: If branch-sensitive OR singleton ZT survives, the source-law preimage is not uniquely promoted yet.
interpretation.provenance_warning: If zero-twisted rows are still only recovered from fallback zero-split, provenance must be tightened before closure.
interpretation.not_closure: This remains below Gap A closure until source-law provenance and global chamber/register lift are established.
